summary: DHH argues that the era of single-page application dominance in web development is ending, creating an opening for Hotwire (HTML Over The Wire) as an alternative approach. He draws parallels between the current moment and Rails' emergence in the mid-2000s, noting that both arose from developer frustration with the prevailing paradigm. He frames the SPA boom as a necessary period of overuse that helped identify the approach's limits. With Rails 7 shipping Hotwire as its default front-end framework, DHH sees the timing as ideal for developers disillusioned with SPA complexity to embrace a simpler model.
key_points: The SPA dominance has peaked, and while SPAs won't disappear, their monopolization of developer attention is over
New approaches must be overused before we can identify their boundaries of fitness — infatuation is what the industry does
Developer discontent with SPAs mirrors the frustration with Java/PHP that fueled Rails adoption in the mid-2000s
Hotwire is shipping as the default front-end approach in Rails 7, positioning it to capture the growing counter-SPA sentiment
Timing matters — a countermelody to SPAs couldn't resonate in 2015, but enough developers have now tried and rejected the approach
money_quotes: New approaches must be overused before we can identify the borders of fitness.

Infatuation is what this industry does. And while that has its drawbacks with the constant churn, the tribalism, and the amnesia of solutions past, it's also a tremendous source of energy and experimentation.
The seeds of interest are sown with the discontent of the immediate past.
A countermelody to the hegemony of single-page application techniques just couldn't resonate in 2015.
The butterfly of an idea flaps its wings years prior, and suddenly a hurricane of interest emerges in the present.
Writing and updating HTML like that never felt like an appropriate solution for the kind of apps I was interested in writing.
key_insight: Technology adoption follows predictable cycles of infatuation and disillusionment, and the post-SPA hangover has created the perfect moment for simpler alternatives like Hotwire to gain traction.
